summary: Portuguese project setup with security and Playwright

message.content: Olá! Por favor, verifique o hasness descrito no CLAUDE.md e continue com a próxima tarefa, ao final, emita um alerta e aguarde - ignore o contexto na pasta ./packages

content.thinking:
O usuário está me pedindo para seguir o protocolo do harness descrito no CLAUDE.md. Vou seguir o fluxo:

1. Ler claude-progress.txt para entender o estado atual
2. Ler features.json para encontrar a próxima feature (passes: false)
3. Implementar uma feature por vez
4. Testar visualmente com Playwright MCP
5. Fazer commit após passar
6. Atualizar claude-progress.txt
7. Parar e alertar o usuário

Vou começar lendo o claude-progress.txt e features.json.

content.text: Olá! Vou seguir o protocolo do harness. Primeiro, vou ler os arquivos de estado para entender a situação atual.

{
  "project": "Metro Integrated Planning System (MMS)",
  "version": "0.3.0",
  "lastUpdated": "2025-12-16",
  "product": {
    "entry": "apps/canvas",
    "description": "Production React app with semantic zoom (Vite + React 18 + R3F + BlueprintJS 6)"
  },
  "reference": {
    "entry": "design/reference/semantic-zoom-poc.html",
    "description": "Standalone POC for visual reference only"
  },
  "priorityQueue": {
    "description": "Features ordered by execution priority. Execute in order, respecting blockedBy dependencies.",
    "note": "Priority 0 = immediate, Priority 1 = high, Priority 2 = after dependencies"
  },
  "features": [
    {
      "id": "HARNESS-001",
      "category": "harness",
      "priority": 0,
      "description": "Configure Playwright MCP for headless without focus stealing",
      "steps": [
        "Research Playwright headless configuration options",
        "Update MCP settings for headless mode",
        "Test that browser windows don't steal focus during visual tests",
        "Document configuration in CLAUDE.md"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": null,
      "notes": "COMPLETE - Playwright MCP configured with --headless flag in .mcp.json. Deny permissions added to block access to personal directories."
    },
    {
      "id": "QA-PLAN-001",
      "category": "harness",
      "priority": 0,
      "description": "Plan QA testing protocol with deep thinking (planning mode)",
      "steps": [
        "Enter planning mode with deep thinking enabled",
        "Research QA best practices for canvas/WebGL applications",
        "Design protocol to enforce QA tests AFTER visual tests",
        "Define QA checklist based on UX-AUDIT-001 discoveries",
        "Update harness hooks to enforce QA step",
        "Document in docs/QA_PROTOCOL.md"
      ],
      "passes": false,
      "blockedBy": "HARNESS-001",
      "notes": "PLANNING TASK - Use planning mode with deep thinking to design mandatory QA testing protocol. QA tests should run after visual tests pass. Informed by UX research."
    },
    {
      "id": "UX-AUDIT-001",
      "category": "research",
      "priority": 1,
      "description": "UX Best Practices Deep Research & Documentation",
      "steps": [
        "Enter planning mode with deep thinking",
        "Study UI patterns: Canva, Figma, Google Maps, iOS, Android",
        "Document all mouse events (click, dblclick, drag, hover, wheel, context)",
        "Document all touch events (tap, pinch, swipe, long-press)",
        "Research cross-platform differences (macOS/Windows/Linux scroll)",
        "Research hardware differences (trackpad vs mouse)",
        "Create docs/UX_RESEARCH.md with all findings",
        "Update harness to preserve context across sessions"
      ],
      "passes": false,
      "blockedBy": "HARNESS-001",
      "notes": "CRITICAL RESEARCH - Deep dive into UX patterns from industry leaders. Informs F118-F121 implementation."
    },
    {
      "id": "F118",
      "category": "ux",
      "priority": 2,
      "description": "Momentum/inertia panning (velocity-based drag)",
      "steps": [
        "Track drag velocity during mouse movement",
        "On mouse release, continue panning with velocity",
        "Apply friction to gradually stop",
        "Test on macOS, Windows (if available)",
        "Match Google Maps/iOS feel"
      ],
      "passes": false,
      "blockedBy": "UX-AUDIT-001",
      "notes": "UX enhancement - drag release continues with velocity and friction, like Google Maps/iOS."
    },
    {
      "id": "F119",
      "category": "ux",
      "priority": 2,
      "description": "Smooth zoom UX (iOS/Google Maps feel)",
      "steps": [
        "Add easing to zoom transitions",
        "Implement zoom momentum",
        "Add pinch-to-zoom support for touch devices",
        "Smooth out zoom steps",
        "Test across different input devices"
      ],
      "passes": false,
      "blockedBy": "UX-AUDIT-001",
      "notes": "UX enhancement - improve zoom feeling with easing, momentum, pinch-to-zoom support."
    },
    {
      "id": "F120",
      "category": "ux",
      "priority": 2,
      "description": "Double-click to zoom in (BUG FIX)",
      "steps": [
        "Add dblclick event handler to canvas",
        "Calculate click position in world coordinates",
        "Zoom 2x centered on click position",
        "Animate zoom transition smoothly",
        "Respect MAX_ZOOM limit"
      ],
      "passes": false,
      "blockedBy": "UX-AUDIT-001",
      "notes": "BUG: Double-click zoom not implemented in production app (was working in POC F004)."
    },
    {
      "id": "F121",
      "category": "ux",
      "priority": 2,
      "description": "Cross-platform scroll/input sensitivity normalization",
      "steps": [
        "Research deltaY values across browsers/OS",
        "Normalize scroll sensitivity for macOS/Windows/Linux",
        "Handle trackpad vs mouse differences",
        "Implement adaptive sensitivity based on input type",
        "Test on multiple platforms"
      ],
      "passes": false,
      "blockedBy": "UX-AUDIT-001",
      "notes": "REQUIRED: Normalize scroll sensitivity across all OS and hardware types."
    },
    {
      "id": "F116",
      "category": "performance",
      "priority": 3,
      "description": "Performance optimization (60fps)",
      "steps": [
        "Profile with Chrome DevTools",
        "Maintain 60fps during pan/zoom",
        "No memory leaks on extended use",
        "InstancedMesh optimizations",
        "Frustum culling for off-screen tasks"
      ],
      "passes": false,
      "blockedBy": "F110",
      "notes": "Critical for production quality. Can be worked on in parallel with UX features."
    },
    {
      "id": "F117",
      "category": "canvas",
      "priority": 4,
      "description": "Full visual parity with POC reference",
      "steps": [
        "Compare screenshots with design/reference/semantic-zoom-poc.html",
        "All 3 LOD layers match visually",
        "Pan/zoom behavior identical",
        "Sidebar and timeline sync correctly",
        "Conflict indicators match"
      ],
      "passes": false,
      "blockedBy": "F116",
      "notes": "Final validation - POC as visual reference"
    },
    {
      "id": "F001",
      "category": "core",
      "description": "Canvas renders and initializes without errors",
      "steps": [
        "Open design/reference/semantic-zoom-poc.html in browser",
        "Canvas displays with header, sidebar, and main area",
        "No JavaScript errors in console",
        "Mock data generates 50 trains with 10 years of tasks"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": null,
      "notes": "POC validated semantic zoom concept. Production app: apps/canvas"
    },
    {
      "id": "F002",
      "category": "navigation",
      "description": "Pan navigation with drag",
      "steps": [
        "Click and drag on canvas to pan",
        "Cursor changes to grabbing while dragging",
        "Pan works in all directions (X and Y)",
        "Sidebar and timeline header sync with pan position"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F001",
      "notes": "Implemented with mousedown/mousemove/mouseup events"
    },
    {
      "id": "F003",
      "category": "navigation",
      "description": "Zoom navigation with scroll wheel",
      "steps": [
        "Scroll up to zoom in, scroll down to zoom out",
        "Zoom centers on mouse cursor position",
        "Zoom respects MIN_ZOOM (0.1x) and MAX_ZOOM (5x) limits",
        "Zoom level indicator updates in header"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F001",
      "notes": "Zoom toward mouse position for natural feel"
    },
    {
      "id": "F004",
      "category": "navigation",
      "description": "Double-click zoom",
      "steps": [
        "Double-click zooms in 2x at click location",
        "Works at any current zoom level (respects max)",
        "Animation is smooth (no jarring jump)"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F003",
      "notes": "Quick zoom in gesture"
    },
    {
      "id": "F005",
      "category": "navigation",
      "description": "Zoom button controls",
      "steps": [
        "Plus button zooms in by 1.5x",
        "Minus button zooms out by 1.5x",
        "Reset button returns to zoom 1.0 and pan origin",
        "Buttons zoom toward center of viewport"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F003",
      "notes": "Alternative for users without scroll wheel"
    },
    {
      "id": "F006",
      "category": "lod",
      "description": "Macro layer renders at zoom < 0.5x",
      "steps": [
        "Zoom out below 0.5x to trigger Macro view",
        "Heatmap displays with Yellow-Orange-Red gradient",
        "Cell density reflects task count per month/train",
        "LOD indicator shows 'MACRO' with yellow badge"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F003",
      "notes": "Strategic 10-20 year overview"
    },
    {
      "id": "F007",
      "category": "lod",
      "description": "Meso layer renders at zoom 0.5x-2x",
      "steps": [
        "Zoom to 0.5x-2x range to trigger Meso view",
        "Task blocks render as colored rectangles (DNA strips)",
        "Alternating row backgrounds for readability",
        "LOD indicator shows 'MESO' with blue badge"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F003",
      "notes": "Tactical 2-5 year view with task blocks"
    },
    {
      "id": "F008",
      "category": "lod",
      "description": "Micro layer renders at zoom > 2x",
      "steps": [
        "Zoom above 2x to trigger Micro view",
        "Task cards render with shadows and rounded corners",
        "Grid lines show days (vertical) and trains (horizontal)",
        "Task type labels visible when card is wide enough",
        "LOD indicator shows 'MICRO' with green badge"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F003",
      "notes": "Operational 4-8 week view with detailed cards"
    },
    {
      "id": "F009",
      "category": "lod",
      "description": "LOD transitions are smooth",
      "steps": [
        "Zoom from Macro to Meso - no flickering",
        "Zoom from Meso to Micro - no flickering",
        "State persists correctly across transitions",
        "Performance stays smooth during transition"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F006",
      "notes": "Critical for Google Earth-like UX feel"
    },
    {
      "id": "F010",
      "category": "ui",
      "description": "Sidebar displays train IDs synced with canvas",
      "steps": [
        "Train IDs visible in left sidebar",
        "IDs update as canvas pans vertically",
        "Row height matches canvas zoom level",
        "Only visible trains are rendered (virtual scroll)"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F002",
      "notes": "Sidebar syncs with panY position"
    },
    {
      "id": "F011",
      "category": "ui",
      "description": "Timeline header displays dates synced with canvas",
      "steps": [
        "Timeline labels update as canvas pans horizontally",
        "Granularity changes based on zoom (years/months/weeks/days)",
        "Labels are readable and don't overlap",
        "Timeline syncs with panX position"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F002",
      "notes": "Adaptive granularity: years at low zoom, days at high zoom"
    },
    {
      "id": "F012",
      "category": "visual",
      "description": "Conflict indicators visible on tasks",
      "steps": [
        "Tasks with conflicts show red border/glow",
        "Conflicts visible in Meso layer (red stroke)",
        "Conflicts visible in Micro layer (red glow shadow)",
        "~5% of tasks have conflict flag for testing"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F007",
      "notes": "Red highlight draws attention to scheduling issues"
    },
    {
      "id": "F013",
      "category": "visual",
      "description": "Task colors match design specifications",
      "steps": [
        "Maintenance tasks: Blue gradient (#3b82f6 to #2563eb)",
        "Overhaul tasks: Orange gradient (#f59e0b to #d97706)",
        "Inspection tasks: Gray gradient (#9ca3af to #6b7280)",
        "Gradients render correctly at all zoom levels"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F007",
      "notes": "Color coding matches docs/UX_SPECIFICATION.md"
    },
    {
      "id": "F014",
      "category": "ux",
      "description": "Onboarding instructions display and auto-hide",
      "steps": [
        "Instructions tooltip visible on page load",
        "Shows: 'Scroll to zoom - Drag to pan - Double-click to zoom in'",
        "Auto-fades after 5 seconds",
        "Non-intrusive (pointer-events: none)"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F001",
      "notes": "Progressive disclosure - teach on first use"
    },
    {
      "id": "F015",
      "category": "ux",
      "description": "LOD legend explains zoom levels",
      "steps": [
        "Legend panel visible in bottom-left corner",
        "Shows Macro (yellow), Meso (blue), Micro (green) colors",
        "Describes zoom thresholds (<0.5x, 0.5-2x, >2x)",
        "Helps users understand semantic zoom concept"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F001",
      "notes": "Educational UI element"
    },
    {
      "id": "F101",
      "category": "canvas",
      "description": "Create Vite + React 18 + BlueprintJS app skeleton",
      "steps": [
        "Create apps/canvas directory with Vite config",
        "Install React 18.3.1, BlueprintJS 6.1.0",
        "Create basic App.tsx with BlueprintJS Navbar",
        "App runs on localhost:5173 without errors",
        "BlueprintJS styles load correctly"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": null,
      "notes": "Foundation for production app - React 18 required for BlueprintJS 6"
    },
    {
      "id": "F102",
      "category": "canvas",
      "description": "Add R3F Canvas with OrthographicCamera",
      "steps": [
        "Install @react-three/fiber and @react-three/drei",
        "Create InfiniteCanvas component with R3F Canvas",
        "Setup OrthographicCamera for 2D-like view",
        "Canvas fills available space below navbar",
        "No WebGL errors in console"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F101",
      "notes": "R3F 8.x compatible with React 18. Verified via Playwright MCP 2025-12-15."
    },
    {
      "id": "F103",
      "category": "canvas",
      "description": "Basic BlueprintJS layout (Navbar, sidebar placeholder)",
      "steps": [
        "Navbar with app title and zoom indicator",
        "Left sidebar container for train IDs",
        "Main area for R3F canvas",
        "Layout uses flexbox, fills viewport",
        "Responsive to window resize"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F102",
      "notes": "BlueprintJS Card, Navbar, ButtonGroup. Sidebar syncs with canvas panY. Verified via Playwright MCP 2025-12-15."
    },
    {
      "id": "F104",
      "category": "canvas",
      "description": "Data types and mock generator",
      "steps": [
        "Create Train, Task, Config TypeScript interfaces",
        "Implement generateData() function",
        "Generate 50 trains with 10 years of tasks",
        "~5% tasks have conflict flag",
        "Data available via React context or store"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F101",
      "notes": "types.ts + generator.ts in apps/canvas/src/data/. Persistent types use ISO strings, render types use numeric offsets."
    },
    {
      "id": "F105",
      "category": "canvas",
      "description": "MesoLayer - InstancedMesh task blocks",
      "steps": [
        "Create MesoLayer component using InstancedMesh",
        "Render task blocks as colored rectangles",
        "Colors: blue (maintenance), orange (overhaul), gray (inspection)",
        "Alternating row backgrounds for readability",
        "Tasks visible at default zoom (1.0x)"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F104",
      "notes": "Implemented with InstancedMesh. Fixed architectural issue: camera init now uses R3F size (not container div). Tasks aligned with sidebar. Verified via Playwright MCP 2025-12-16."
    },
    {
      "id": "F106",
      "category": "canvas",
      "description": "Pan and Zoom camera controls",
      "steps": [
        "Drag to pan (mousedown + mousemove)",
        "Scroll to zoom (wheel event)",
        "Zoom centers on mouse cursor",
        "Respect MIN_ZOOM (0.1) and MAX_ZOOM (5.0)",
        "Cursor changes to grab/grabbing"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F102",
      "notes": "Pan bounded to world limits. Zoom via wheel and buttons. Camera initialized using R3F size (not container div). Verified via Playwright MCP 2025-12-16."
    },
    {
      "id": "F107",
      "category": "canvas",
      "description": "LOD state management",
      "steps": [
        "Create useViewport hook with zoom, panX, panY state",
        "Compute LOD from zoom: MACRO (<0.5), MESO (0.5-2), MICRO (>2)",
        "LOD changes trigger layer visibility",
        "State persists across renders",
        "Export state for UI components"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F102",
      "notes": "Implemented in App.tsx (getLOD, zoom/panY state). Hook extraction optional. Layer visibility tested when F105+ exist."
    },
    {
      "id": "F108",
      "category": "canvas",
      "description": "MacroLayer - Heatmap cells",
      "steps": [
        "Create MacroLayer component",
        "Render heatmap cells based on task density",
        "Color gradient: yellow -> orange -> red",
        "Only visible when zoom < 0.5x",
        "Smooth transition from MesoLayer"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F105",
      "notes": "Implemented with InstancedMesh. Task density aggregated by month. Color gradient: light yellow -> amber -> orange -> red. Year markers included. Verified via Playwright MCP 2025-12-16."
    },
    {
      "id": "F109",
      "category": "canvas",
      "description": "MicroLayer - Html task cards with labels",

   416→## Session: 2025-12-16 (F105/F106 Architectural Fix - Camera Alignment)
   417→
   418→### Problem: Tasks Not Aligned with Sidebar
   419→
   420→**Symptoms:**
   421→- Tasks appeared in center-right of canvas instead of left edge
   422→- Tasks didn't align with train rows in sidebar
   423→- Camera could be dragged infinitely (no bounds)
   424→
   425→**Root Cause Analysis:**
   426→- InfiniteCanvas used `containerRef.getBoundingClientRect()` to measure canvas size
   427→- R3F's actual canvas size (from `useThree().size`) was different due to flexbox layout
   428→- Container div measured: 800x600
   429→- Actual R3F canvas: 1070x679
   430→- Camera was positioned incorrectly: at (400, -300) instead of (535, -339.5)
   431→- This caused viewport to show X from -135 to 935 instead of 0 to 1070
   432→
   433→**Solution:**
   434→- Created `CameraController` component that runs INSIDE the R3F Canvas context
   435→- Uses `useThree().size` to get actual canvas dimensions
   436→- Initializes camera position correctly on first render
   437→- Reports actual size back to parent for pan/zoom calculations
   438→
   439→**Files Changed:**
   440→- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` - Complete refactor
   441→  - Removed old `CameraSync` component
   442→  - Added new `CameraController` with proper R3F size handling
   443→  - Camera initialization now happens inside R3F context
   444→  - Pan/zoom handlers use correct canvas dimensions
   445→- `apps/canvas/src/data/types.ts` - Added helper functions
   446→  - `calculateDataBounds()` for computing task extent
   447→  - `calculateFitToView()` for D3.js-style fit-to-bounds
   448→
   449→**Visual Tests (Playwright MCP):**
   450→- ✅ Tasks aligned with train rows (TRAIN-001 = first task row)
   451→- ✅ Tasks start near left edge of canvas
   452→- ✅ Pan bounded to world limits (can't drag infinitely)
   453→- ✅ Zoom works correctly (+/- buttons, wheel)
   454→- ✅ Reset View returns to initial state
   455→- ✅ Conflict indicators visible (red tasks)
   456→- ✅ No console errors
   457→
   458→**Key Insight:**
   459→When using R3F, always get canvas dimensions from `useThree().size`, not from the container div. The container and canvas can have different sizes due to CSS layout (flexbox, padding, borders).
   460→
   461→**Features Updated:**
   462→- F105: ✅ Passes (tasks aligned with sidebar)
   463→- F106: ✅ Passes (pan/zoom with correct bounds)
   464→- F114: ✅ Passes (conflict indicators already working)
   465→
   466→**Current State:**
   467→- F101-F107: ✅ Foundation + MesoLayer + Pan/Zoom complete

   469→- F111-F114: ✅ Complete (sidebar, timeline partial, controls, conflicts)

   721→## Session: 2025-12-15 (Harness Self-Awareness System)
   722→
   723→### Meta-Reflection: Why Claude Didn't Understand the Harness
   724→
   725→**Problem:** Claude Code was starting sessions without understanding what "Harness" is, despite existing documentation.
   726→
   727→**Root Cause Analysis:**
   728→1. **Poor positioning:** Harness section was at line 293 of CLAUDE.md (after 200+ lines of other content)
   729→2. **Hook assumed prior knowledge:** `session-start.sh` said "what to do" but not "what is" or "why"
   730→3. **No research fallback:** No links to official Anthropic documentation
   731→4. **No self-correction mechanism:** No instruction for Claude to fix the harness if it doesn't understand
   732→
   733→**Solution Implemented:**
   734→
   735→1. **Updated `session-start.sh`:**
   736→   - Added "WHAT IS A HARNESS?" section with definition
   737→   - Added "WORK PROTOCOL" section with steps
   738→   - Added "SELF-CORRECTION PROTOCOL" section with instructions to:
   739→     - Search Anthropic docs if not understood
   740→     - Analyze why local docs failed
   741→     - Fix CLAUDE.md or hooks
   742→     - Document in progress.txt
   743→
   744→2. **Updated `CLAUDE.md`:**
   745→   - Moved harness section to BEGINNING (right after Project Overview)
   746→   - Added official Anthropic link
   747→   - Added Self-Correction Protocol
   748→   - Removed duplicate section from line 293
   749→
   750→**Key Innovation:** Self-correction loop where each session leaves the harness clearer for the next.
   751→
   752→**Sources:**
   753→- [Effective harnesses for long-running agents](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents)
   754→- [Claude Code Hooks Guide](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks-guide)

   873→## Session: 2025-12-15 (Claude Code Hooks)
   874→
   875→### Implemented Harness Enforcement via Hooks
   876→
   877→Based on:
   878→- [Effective harnesses for long-running agents](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents)
   879→- [Claude Code Hooks Guide](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks-guide)
   880→
   881→**Hooks Created:**
   882→- [x] `session-start.sh` (SessionStart) - Harness protocol reminders + package versions

# Metro Integrated Planning System - Progress Log

## Session: 2025-12-16 (HARNESS-001 - Security & Playwright Headless) - COMPLETE ✅

### HARNESS-001: Configure Playwright MCP headless + Security Permissions

**Problem Identified:**
- Playwright MCP was running in headed mode, stealing focus during visual tests
- No protection against accessing personal directories (~/Desktop, ~/Documents, etc.)
- A `find ~` command triggered macOS access alerts

**Solution Implemented:**

1. **Playwright MCP Headless Configuration:**
   - Created `.mcp.json` with `--headless` flag
   - Browser now runs invisibly without stealing focus
   - Added `.mcp.json` to `.gitignore` (user-specific config)

2. **Deny Permissions for Personal Directories:**
   - Added deny rules to `.claude/settings.local.json`
   - Blocked: `~/Desktop`, `~/Documents`, `~/Downloads`, `~/Pictures`, `~/Movies`, `~/Music`
   - Blocked: `find ~`, `find /Users` commands
   - Protection applies to: Read, Write, Edit, Glob, Grep, Bash operations

**Files Changed:**
- `.mcp.json` (NEW - Playwright headless config)
- `.claude/settings.local.json` (deny permissions added)
- `CLAUDE.md` (Security & Permissions section added)
- `features.json` (HARNESS-001 passes: true)

**Visual Test:**
- ✅ Playwright navigated to localhost:5175
- ✅ Screenshot captured without popup window
- ✅ App renders correctly (MESO view, 50 trains, timeline)

**Next:** QA-PLAN-001 (Plan QA testing protocol) or UX-AUDIT-001 (UX research)

---

## Session: 2025-12-16 (Harness Restructure) - COMPLETE ✅

### Harness Restructure: Priority Queue Implementation

**Problem Identified:**
- Priority tasks (HARNESS-001, UX-AUDIT-001, F118-F121) were buried in `archive.deferredFeatures`
- No clear execution order for pending features
- Missing QA testing protocol planning task

**Solution Implemented:**

1. **Moved priority features to active list (top of features array):**
   - HARNESS-001 (priority 0): Configure Playwright headless
   - QA-PLAN-001 (priority 0, NEW): Plan QA testing protocol with deep thinking
   - UX-AUDIT-001 (priority 1): UX Best Practices Research
   - F118 (priority 2): Momentum/inertia panning
   - F119 (priority 2): Smooth zoom UX
   - F120 (priority 2): Double-click zoom (BUG FIX)
   - F121 (priority 2): Cross-platform scroll sensitivity
   - F116 (priority 3): Performance optimization
   - F117 (priority 4): Visual parity

2. **Added `priorityQueue` section to features.json:**
   - Priority 0 = immediate execution
   - Priority 1 = high priority
   - Priority 2+ = after dependencies

3. **Added `testingProtocol` section:**
   - Defines mandatory sequence: VISUAL TEST → CONSOLE CHECK → QA TEST → MARK COMPLETE
   - QA protocol to be defined in QA-PLAN-001

4. **Added QA-PLAN-001 task:**
   - Planning task using deep thinking mode
   - Design protocol to enforce QA tests AFTER visual tests
   - Based on UX-AUDIT-001 discoveries
   - Output: docs/QA_PROTOCOL.md + harness hooks

**Files Changed:**
- `features.json` (restructured with priority queue)
- `claude-progress.txt` (this update)

**Current Execution Order:**
```
1. HARNESS-001: Playwright headless (blockedBy: null)
2. QA-PLAN-001: QA protocol planning (blockedBy: HARNESS-001)
3. UX-AUDIT-001: UX research (blockedBy: HARNESS-001)
4. F118-F121: UX enhancements (blockedBy: UX-AUDIT-001)
5. F116: Performance (blockedBy: F110, can parallel with UX)
6. F117: Visual parity (blockedBy: F116)
```

**Next:** Execute HARNESS-001 (Configure Playwright headless)

### F115: Instructions Toaster with auto-dismiss - COMPLETE

**Implemented:**
- Added BlueprintJS OverlayToaster to App.tsx
- Shows instruction toast on first load: "🖱️ Scroll to zoom • Drag to pan • Double-click to zoom in"
- Auto-dismisses after 5 seconds

## Session: 2025-12-16 (F112 - Timeline Header) - COMPLETE ✅

### F112: Timeline header with adaptive granularity - COMPLETE

**Implemented:**
- Created `apps/canvas/src/components/TimelineHeader.tsx` component
- Adaptive granularity based on zoom level:
  - < 0.5x: Years
  - 0.5-1.5x: Months (e.g., "Jan 2024", "Feb 2024")
  - 1.5-3.0x: Weeks (e.g., "Jan 7", "Jan 14")
  - > 3.0x: Days (e.g., "Mon, Jan 1", "Tue, Jan 2")
- Updated InfiniteCanvas to export `ViewportInfo` with `leftX`, `rightX`, `topY`
- Labels sync with horizontal pan position
- Gradient overlays fade labels at edges
- Tick marks below each label

**Technical Details:**
- Uses `pxPerDay` constant (10px) to calculate date positions
- Generates labels dynamically based on visible viewport range
- Date formatting uses browser's `toLocaleDateString()` API

**Bug Fixed:**
- Vite "does not provide an export named" error with TypeScript interfaces
- Root cause: esbuild removes type exports during dev mode transformation
- Solution: Use `import type { ViewportInfo }` instead of `import { ViewportInfo }`
- Reference: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/issues/2117

**Files Changed:**
- `apps/canvas/src/components/TimelineHeader.tsx` (NEW)
- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (ViewportInfo export, viewport X reporting)
- `apps/canvas/src/App.tsx` (import TimelineHeader, viewport state)
- `features.json` (F112 passes: true)

**Visual Tests (Playwright MCP):**
- ✅ Months at 1.0x zoom: "Jan 2024", "Feb 2024", "Mar 2024"
- ✅ Weeks at 1.5x zoom: "Jan 7", "Jan 14", "Jan 21"
- ✅ Days at 3.38x zoom: "Mon, Jan 1", "Tue, Jan 2", "Wed, Jan 3"
- ✅ Labels sync with horizontal pan
- ✅ No console errors

**Commit:** `735efce`

**Current State:**
- F101-F111, F113-F114: ✅ Complete
- F112: ✅ Complete (Timeline header)
- F115: ❌ Pending (Instructions Toaster)
- F116-F117: ❌ Pending (Performance, visual parity)

**Next:** F115 (Instructions Toaster) or continue with polish features

---

## Session: 2025-12-16 (Performance Optimization) - COMPLETE ✅

### Performance Issues Resolved

**Problems Fixed:**
1. ✅ Zoom lagging/slow at high zoom - fixed with frustum culling
2. ✅ Labels only appearing after seconds - fixed with lazy rendering (max 50 labels)
3. ✅ White space around graph at minimum zoom - fixed with dynamic MIN_ZOOM
4. ✅ Sidebar train list scrambled at small zoom - fixed by limiting zoom to ~0.57x

**Optimizations Implemented:**

#### 1. Frustum Culling (MicroLayer.tsx)
- Added `filterVisibleTasks()` function to only render tasks within viewport bounds
- Added 100px padding for smooth panning
- Reduced rendering from ~5,500 tasks to only visible ones (~50-200)

#### 2. InstancedMesh for Grid Lines (MicroLayer.tsx)
- Converted 251 individual meshes to 2 InstancedMesh (horizontal + vertical lines)
- Reduced draw calls from 251 to 2
- Grid lines also use frustum culling based on viewport

#### 3. InstancedMesh for Row Backgrounds (MicroLayer.tsx)
- Converted 50 individual meshes to 1 InstancedMesh
- Reduced draw calls from 50 to 1
- Only renders visible rows based on viewport

#### 4. Lazy Label Rendering (MicroLayer.tsx)
- Limited HTML labels to max 50 (sorted by task width, largest first)
- Labels only render when opacity > 0.3 (performance optimization)
- Labels only for visible tasks within viewport

#### 5. Dynamic MIN_ZOOM (InfiniteCanvas.tsx + App.tsx)
- Added `effectiveMinZoom` calculation based on canvas size and world bounds
- Formula: `minZoom = max(canvasWidth/worldWidth, canvasHeight/worldHeight)`
- Added `onMinZoomChange` callback to sync with App.tsx
- Now zoom stops at ~0.57x instead of 0.10x, preventing white space

**Files Changed:**
- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (ViewportBounds, effectiveMinZoom, onMinZoomChange)
- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/layers/MicroLayer.tsx` (complete rewrite with optimizations)
- `apps/canvas/src/App.tsx` (minZoom state, handleMinZoomChange)

**Visual Tests (Playwright MCP):**
- ✅ MESO view at 1.0x - task blocks render correctly
- ✅ MICRO view at 2.25x - ~50 labels visible (not 2000), grid lines visible
- ✅ MACRO view at 0.44x - heatmap renders correctly
- ✅ Dynamic MIN_ZOOM at 0.57x - content fills viewport, no excessive white space
- ✅ Sidebar readable at minimum zoom (not scrambled)
- ✅ No console errors

**Performance Impact:**
| Metric | Before | After | Improvement |
|--------|--------|-------|-------------|
| Grid Lines Draw Calls | 251 | 2 | 99.2% reduction |
| Row Background Draw Calls | 50 | 1 | 98% reduction |

## Session: 2025-12-16 (F110 - Smooth LOD Transitions)

### F110: Smooth LOD transitions - COMPLETE

**Implemented:**
- Blend zones (±0.15) around LOD thresholds for smooth crossfade
- `calculateLayerOpacities()` function to compute opacity for each layer based on zoom
- `LODLayers` component to orchestrate layer rendering with opacity
- All three layers (Macro/Meso/Micro) now accept `opacity` prop

**Technical Details:**
- **LOD Thresholds:** MACRO < 0.5x, MESO 0.5-2x, MICRO > 2x

  - MACRO → MESO: blend zone 0.35-0.65
  - MESO → MICRO: blend zone 1.85-2.15
- During blend zones, both adjacent layers render simultaneously with interpolated opacity
- All Three.js materials use `transparent={opacity < 1}` for proper blending

## Session: 2025-12-16 (F105/F106 Architectural Fix - Camera Alignment)

### Problem: Tasks Not Aligned with Sidebar

**Symptoms:**
- Tasks appeared in center-right of canvas instead of left edge
- Tasks didn't align with train rows in sidebar
- Camera could be dragged infinitely (no bounds)

**Root Cause Analysis:**
- InfiniteCanvas used `containerRef.getBoundingClientRect()` to measure canvas size
- R3F's actual canvas size (from `useThree().size`) was different due to flexbox layout
- Container div measured: 800x600
- Actual R3F canvas: 1070x679
- Camera was positioned incorrectly: at (400, -300) instead of (535, -339.5)
- This caused viewport to show X from -135 to 935 instead of 0 to 1070

**Solution:**
- Created `CameraController` component that runs INSIDE the R3F Canvas context
- Uses `useThree().size` to get actual canvas dimensions
- Initializes camera position correctly on first render
- Reports actual size back to parent for pan/zoom calculations

**Files Changed:**
- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` - Complete refactor
  - Removed old `CameraSync` component
  - Added new `CameraController` with proper R3F size handling
  - Camera initialization now happens inside R3F context
  - Pan/zoom handlers use correct canvas dimensions
- `apps/canvas/src/data/types.ts` - Added helper functions
  - `calculateDataBounds()` for computing task extent
  - `calculateFitToView()` for D3.js-style fit-to-bounds

**Visual Tests (Playwright MCP):**
- ✅ Tasks aligned with train rows (TRAIN-001 = first task row)
- ✅ Tasks start near left edge of canvas
- ✅ Pan bounded to world limits (can't drag infinitely)
- ✅ Zoom works correctly (+/- buttons, wheel)
- ✅ Reset View returns to initial state
- ✅ Conflict indicators visible (red tasks)
- ✅ No console errors

**Key Insight:**
When using R3F, always get canvas dimensions from `useThree().size`, not from the container div. The container and canvas can have different sizes due to CSS layout (flexbox, padding, borders).

**Features Updated:**
- F105: ✅ Passes (tasks aligned with sidebar)
- F106: ✅ Passes (pan/zoom with correct bounds)
- F114: ✅ Passes (conflict indicators already working)

**Current State:**
- F101-F107: ✅ Foundation + MesoLayer + Pan/Zoom complete
- F108-F110: ❌ Pending (MacroLayer, MicroLayer, transitions)
- F111-F114: ✅ Complete (sidebar, timeline partial, controls, conflicts)
- F115-F117: ❌ Pending (polish)

## Session: 2025-12-15 (Harness Self-Awareness System)

### Meta-Reflection: Why Claude Didn't Understand the Harness

**Problem:** Claude Code was starting sessions without understanding what "Harness" is, despite existing documentation.

**Root Cause Analysis:**
1. **Poor positioning:** Harness section was at line 293 of CLAUDE.md (after 200+ lines of other content)
2. **Hook assumed prior knowledge:** `session-start.sh` said "what to do" but not "what is" or "why"
3. **No research fallback:** No links to official Anthropic documentation
4. **No self-correction mechanism:** No instruction for Claude to fix the harness if it doesn't understand

**Solution Implemented:**

1. **Updated `session-start.sh`:**
   - Added "WHAT IS A HARNESS?" section with definition
   - Added "WORK PROTOCOL" section with steps
   - Added "SELF-CORRECTION PROTOCOL" section with instructions to:
     - Search Anthropic docs if not understood
     - Analyze why local docs failed
     - Fix CLAUDE.md or hooks
     - Document in progress.txt

2. **Updated `CLAUDE.md`:**
   - Moved harness section to BEGINNING (right after Project Overview)
   - Added official Anthropic link
   - Added Self-Correction Protocol
   - Removed duplicate section from line 293

**Key Innovation:** Self-correction loop where each session leaves the harness clearer for the next.

**Sources:**
- [Effective harnesses for long-running agents](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents)
- [Claude Code Hooks Guide](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks-guide)

### Implemented Harness Enforcement via Hooks

Based on:
- [Effective harnesses for long-running agents](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents)
- [Claude Code Hooks Guide](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks-guide)

**Hooks Created:**
- [x] `session-start.sh` (SessionStart) - Harness protocol reminders + package versions
- [x] `protect-package-json.sh` (PreToolUse) - BLOCKS manual package.json edits
- [x] `after-write.sh` (PostToolUse) - Quick feedback after file edits
- [x] `task-type-detector.py` (UserPromptSubmit) - Task-type aware reminders
- [x] `end-of-turn-check.sh` (Stop) - Quality gate at end of turn

**Architecture:**
```
PreToolUse  → BLOCKING (prevent bad actions)
PostToolUse → FAST (quick feedback)
Stop        → QUALITY GATE (typecheck, visual test reminders)
```

However, I can share some general knowledge about WebGL canvas testing best practices based on my existing knowledge:

## General WebGL Canvas Testing Best Practices

### Visual/Rendering Tests
- **Screenshot comparison testing** - Capture rendered frames and compare against baseline images
- **Pixel-level validation** - Check specific pixel values for expected colors
- **Cross-browser testing** - WebGL implementations vary across browsers (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge)

### Performance Testing
- **Frame rate monitoring** - Ensure consistent FPS under various loads
- **Memory leak detection** - Monitor GPU memory usage over time
- **Draw call optimization** - Track and limit draw calls

### Functional Testing
- **Context loss/restoration** - Test how your app handles `webglcontextlost` and `webglcontextrestored` events
- **Shader compilation validation** - Verify shaders compile without errors
- **Extension availability checks** - Test fallbacks when WebGL extensions aren't available

### Tools Commonly Used
- **Puppeteer/Playwright** - Headless browser testing with GPU support
- **WebGL Inspector** - Debug and profile WebGL calls
- **Spector.js** - Capture and inspect WebGL frames

Would you like me to try the search again, or would you like more details on any of these general practices?

Here are the search results for Playwright visual regression testing with WebGL canvas in 2025:

## Key Findings

### WebGL-Specific Testing with Playwright
Animations are difficult to test, especially technologies like Canvas and webGL.(no content)Testing canvas elements and WebGL animations is a difficult task. To achieve smooth animations and good performance in end-to-end testing – the Playwright framework is a good choice.

**Configuration for WebGL/Headless Testing:**
To enable headless mode, we are going to edit the playwright.config.js file and set the headless param to true and add additional args for browser launch. By default, the browser uses the ANGLE layer to render WebGL draw calls.

The recommended Playwright config for WebGL includes:
```javascript
launchOptions: {
  args: ["--headless","--no-sandbox","--use-angle=gl"]
}
```

Headless tests are executed in 12 seconds and screenshots prove that hardware acceleration is enabled. The Aquarium animation is smooth with 60FPS.

### Docker Considerations
We need to remember that Docker does not utilize hardware acceleration by default. The solution for this is to use the NVIDIA Container Toolkit that allows users to build and run GPU accelerated Docker containers.

### General Visual Regression Testing with Playwright

Playwright Test includes the ability to produce and visually compare screenshots using `await expect(page).toHaveScreenshot()`. On first execution, Playwright test will generate reference screenshots. Subsequent runs will compare against the reference.(no content)Playwright Test uses the pixelmatch library. You can configure options like `maxDiffPixels` to control comparison sensitivity.

### Best Practices

Use Thresholds Wisely: Set an appropriate pixel difference threshold to allow minor acceptable differences, especially for anti-aliasing or rendering quirks across browsers.(no content)Browser rendering can vary based on the host OS, version, settings, hardware, power source (battery vs. power adapter), headless mode, and other factors. For consistent screenshots, run tests in the same environment where the baseline screenshots were generated.(no content)Using Playwright snapshots with mocked data can significantly improve the speed at which UI regression is carried out. It facilitates rapid automated inspection of UI elements across the three main browsers (Chromium, Firefox, Webkit).

content.content:
# WebGL Testing with Playwright: Key Configuration Settings

## Challenge
Testing WebGL animations in headless Chrome requires GPU hardware acceleration to achieve smooth 60fps performance, which is disabled by default in headless mode.

## Critical Configuration Flag

The essential fix involves modifying `playwright.config.js`:

```javascript
launchOptions: {
    args: ["--headless", "--no-sandbox", "--use-angle=gl"]
}
```

**Key difference:** Use `--use-angle=gl` (not `--use-angle=angle`) to force hardware acceleration in headless mode.

## Test Structure Example

```javascript
test("WebGL animation test", async ({ page }) => {
    await page.goto("https://webglsamples.org/aquarium/aquarium.html");
    await waitFor(5000);
    await page.screenshot({ path: 'screens/screenshot.png', fullPage: true });
})
```

## Configuration Best Practices

| Setting | Value | Purpose |
|---------|-------|---------|
| `headless` | `true` | Runs without UI (suitable for CI/CD) |
| `--no-sandbox` | Required | Enables browser launch in restricted environments |
| `--use-angle=gl` | Critical | Activates GPU-accelerated WebGL rendering |
| `timeout` | 30+ seconds | Allows animation capture time |

## Performance Results

With proper configuration:
- **FPS:** 60 frames per second achieved
- **Execution time:** ~12-13 seconds for multiple tests
- **Hardware acceleration:** Confirmed enabled

## Installation Requirements

```bash
npm install @playwright/test@1.27.1
npx playwright install
```

## Docker Consideration

For containerized testing with GPU support, use NVIDIA Container Toolkit to enable hardware acceleration beyond default Docker capabilities.

#!/bin/bash
# Hook: Stop (Quality Gate)
# Purpose: End-of-turn validation - typecheck, visual test reminders
# Exit 2 = BLOCK continuation, Exit 0 = Allow

PROJECT_DIR="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-$(pwd)}"
cd "$PROJECT_DIR" 2>/dev/null || exit 0

echo "━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━"
echo "🔍 END-OF-TURN QUALITY CHECK"
echo "━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━"

# 1. TypeScript check (if .ts/.tsx changed)
TS_CHANGED=false
if git diff --name-only HEAD 2>/dev/null | grep -qE '\.(ts|tsx)$'; then
  TS_CHANGED=true
fi
if git ls-files --others --exclude-standard 2>/dev/null | grep -qE '\.(ts|tsx)$'; then
  TS_CHANGED=true
fi

if [ "$TS_CHANGED" = true ]; then
  echo "📝 TypeScript files changed - running typecheck..."
  if ! bun run typecheck 2>&1 | head -20; then
    echo "" >&2
    echo "❌ TYPECHECK FAILED" >&2
    echo "Fix TypeScript errors before continuing." >&2
    exit 2
  fi
  echo "✅ Typecheck passed"
fi

# 2. Check for canvas app changes (visual test reminder)
CANVAS_CHANGED=false
if git diff --name-only HEAD 2>/dev/null | grep -q 'apps/canvas/'; then
  CANVAS_CHANGED=true
fi
if git ls-files --others --exclude-standard 2>/dev/null | grep -q 'apps/canvas/'; then
  CANVAS_CHANGED=true
fi

if [ "$CANVAS_CHANGED" = true ]; then
  echo ""
  echo "┌────────────────────────────────────────────┐"
  echo "│ 📸 VISUAL TEST REQUIRED                    │"
  echo "│                                            │"
  echo "│ Canvas app changed. Before marking feature │"
  echo "│ complete, run visual test with Playwright: │"
  echo "│                                            │"
  echo "│ 1. mcp__playwright__browser_navigate       │"
  echo "│ 2. mcp__playwright__browser_console_messages│"
  echo "│ 3. mcp__playwright__browser_take_screenshot│"
  echo "│ 4. Analyze screenshot                      │"
  echo "└────────────────────────────────────────────┘"
fi

# 3. Harness file validation
if [ ! -f "features.json" ]; then
  echo "⚠️  features.json not found - harness incomplete" >&2
fi

if [ ! -f "claude-progress.txt" ]; then
  echo "⚠️  claude-progress.txt not found - update progress log" >&2
fi

echo ""
echo "✅ End-of-turn check complete"
exit 0

#!/bin/bash
# Hook: SessionStart - Harness Context Injection + Self-Correction
# Purpose: Ensure Claude understands harness concept at session start
# Always exit 0 (informational only)

PROJECT_DIR="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-$(pwd)}"
cd "$PROJECT_DIR" 2>/dev/null || exit 0

echo "━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━"
echo "🚀 MMS HARNESS - SESSION START"
echo "━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━"

# WHAT IS A HARNESS? (definition FIRST - before any steps)
echo ""
echo "┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐"
echo "│ WHAT IS A HARNESS?                                          │"
echo "├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤"
echo "│ A 'Harness' is a framework that enables AI agents to work   │"
echo "│ on long tasks across multiple context windows/sessions.     │"
echo "│                                                              │"
echo "│ PROBLEM: Each new session starts with NO memory.            │"
echo "│ SOLUTION: State files (progress + features) persist context.│"
echo "│                                                              │"
echo "│ Official source: anthropic.com/engineering/                 │"
echo "│                  effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents│"
echo "└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘"

# Current date (prevents stale training data issue)
echo ""
echo "📅 Current date: $(date '+%Y-%m-%d')"

# Work protocol
echo ""
echo "┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐"
echo "│ WORK PROTOCOL                                               │"
echo "├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤"
echo "│ 1. READ claude-progress.txt → session history              │"
echo "│ 2. READ features.json → next feature (passes:false)        │"
echo "│ 3. IMPLEMENT one feature at a time                          │"
echo "│ 4. TEST visually with Playwright MCP                        │"
echo "│ 5. COMMIT after feature passes                              │"
echo "│ 6. UPDATE claude-progress.txt                               │"
echo "└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘"

# SELF-CORRECTION PROTOCOL (key innovation)
echo ""
echo "┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐"
echo "│ 🔄 SELF-CORRECTION PROTOCOL                                 │"
echo "├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤"
echo "│ If you DON'T KNOW what a 'Harness' is:                      │"
echo "│                                                              │"
echo "│ 1. SEARCH: WebSearch 'Anthropic harness agents 2025'        │"
echo "│ 2. ANALYZE: Why was local documentation unclear?            │"
echo "│ 3. FIX: Update CLAUDE.md or hooks to improve clarity        │"
echo "│ 4. DOCUMENT: Record the fix in claude-progress.txt          │"
echo "│                                                              │"
echo "│ This is a CONTINUOUS IMPROVEMENT system.                    │"
echo "│ Each session should leave harness clearer for the next.     │"
echo "└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘"

# Package versions (helps with stale knowledge detection)
# Note: dependencies are in apps/canvas/package.json, not root
if [ -f "apps/canvas/package.json" ]; then
  echo ""
  echo "📦 Current package versions (apps/canvas):"
  jq -r '.dependencies | to_entries[] | select(.key | test("react|three|blueprint")) | "   \(.key): \(.value)"' apps/canvas/package.json 2>/dev/null || true
fi

# Recent git activity
if git rev-parse --git-dir > /dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo ""
  echo "📜 Recent commits:"
  git --no-pager log --oneline -3 2>/dev/null | sed 's/^/   /'
fi

exit 0

input.content:
# QA Testing Protocol for MMS Canvas

This document defines the mandatory Quality Assurance testing protocol for the MMS Canvas application. All features MUST pass this protocol before being marked as complete.

---

## Overview

The MMS Canvas is a WebGL-based visualization that requires specialized testing approaches beyond standard web app testing. This protocol ensures:

1. Visual correctness (WebGL renders properly)
2. Performance standards (60fps target)
3. Cross-browser compatibility
4. Console error-free execution
5. Consistent results across sessions

---

## Mandatory Test Sequence

Every feature that modifies `apps/canvas/` MUST follow this sequence:

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    QA TEST SEQUENCE                          │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                              │
│  1. TYPECHECK                                                │
│     └── bun run typecheck                                   │
│                                                              │
│  2. VISUAL TEST (Playwright MCP)                            │
│     ├── Navigate to http://localhost:5173                   │
│     ├── Take screenshot                                      │
│     ├── Verify content renders (not blank)                  │
│     └── Check layout matches expectations                    │
│                                                              │
│  3. CONSOLE CHECK                                           │
│     ├── Check for JavaScript errors                         │
│     ├── Check for WebGL warnings                            │
│     └── Check for React errors                              │
│                                                              │
│  4. INTERACTION TEST (if applicable)                        │
│     ├── Pan (drag to move)                                  │
│     ├── Zoom (scroll or buttons)                            │
│     ├── LOD transitions (MACRO ↔ MESO ↔ MICRO)             │
│     └── Sidebar sync                                         │
│                                                              │
│  5. PERFORMANCE CHECK (for F116+)                           │
│     ├── No visible lag during pan/zoom                      │
│     ├── Smooth LOD transitions                              │
│     └── Labels appear without delay                          │
│                                                              │
│  6. MARK COMPLETE                                           │
│     └── Update features.json (passes: true)                 │
│                                                              │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

---

## Playwright MCP Commands

### 1. Start Dev Server

Before running visual tests, ensure the dev server is running:

```bash
cd apps/canvas && bun run dev
```

### 2. Navigate to App

```
mcp__playwright__browser_navigate url="http://localhost:5173"
```

### 3. Check Console for Errors

```
mcp__playwright__browser_console_messages level="error"
```

**Interpretation:**
- Empty result or no red errors → PASS
- Any red errors → FAIL (investigate before continuing)
- Yellow warnings → Evaluate (may be acceptable)

### 4. Take Screenshot

```
mcp__playwright__browser_take_screenshot filename="screenshots/feature-name.png"
```

For full page:
```
mcp__playwright__browser_take_screenshot fullPage=true filename="screenshots/feature-full.png"
```

### 5. Accessibility Snapshot (Optional)

```
mcp__playwright__browser_snapshot
```

Useful for verifying:
- Element presence in DOM
- ARIA labels
- Interactive elements

### 6. Close Browser

```
mcp__playwright__browser_close
```

---

## WebGL-Specific Testing

### Configuration

The project uses Playwright MCP with headless mode configured in `.mcp.json`:

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "playwright": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["@playwright/mcp@latest", "--headless"]
    }
  }
}
```

**Key points:**
- `--headless` prevents browser window from stealing focus
- WebGL acceleration is enabled by default in Chromium
- Screenshots capture WebGL canvas content

### WebGL Verification Checklist

| Check | What to Look For | PASS | FAIL |
|-------|------------------|------|------|
| Canvas renders | Content visible (not blank) | Non-empty canvas | White/black screen |
| LOD layers | Correct layer for zoom level | Expected view type | Wrong layer |
| Colors | Correct task/heatmap colors | Blue/orange/gray/red | Missing or wrong |
| Performance | Smooth interactions | No visible lag | Choppy/slow |
| Console | No WebGL errors | Clean console | Shader/context errors |

### Common WebGL Issues

| Issue | Symptom | Solution |
|-------|---------|----------|
| Blank canvas | Nothing renders | Check InstancedMesh count, camera position |
| Wrong colors | Tasks not visible | Check material/color attributes |
| Shader error | Console shows GLSL error | Fix shader syntax |
| Context lost | Canvas goes blank after time | Handle contextlost event |
| Performance | Choppy zoom/pan | Check frustum culling, instance count |

---

## LOD Transition Testing

Test all three LOD levels and transitions:

### Zoom Levels

| LOD | Zoom Range | Expected View | Verification |
|-----|------------|---------------|--------------|
| MACRO | < 0.5x | Heatmap (yellow→red) | Cell colors visible |
| MESO | 0.5x - 2.0x | Task blocks (DNA pattern) | Colored rectangles |
| MICRO | > 2.0x | Task cards with labels | Grid lines, labels |

### Transition Tests

1. **MESO → MACRO:** Zoom out past 0.5x
   - Both layers should crossfade smoothly
   - No flickering

2. **MESO → MICRO:** Zoom in past 2.0x
   - Both layers should crossfade smoothly
   - Labels appear progressively

3. **Full range:** Zoom from 0.3x to 3.0x
   - All transitions smooth
   - No visual artifacts

---

## Approval Criteria Matrix

### Visual Tests

| Criterion | PASS | FAIL |
|-----------|------|------|
| Content renders | Canvas shows expected content | Blank/empty screen |
| Correct LOD | Layer matches zoom level | Wrong layer displayed |
| Colors correct | Task colors match type | Missing or wrong colors |
| Layout correct | Sidebar, header, canvas aligned | Misaligned elements |
| No artifacts | Clean rendering | Glitches, overlaps |

### Console Tests

| Criterion | PASS | FAIL |
|-----------|------|------|
| No JS errors | No red errors | Any red error |
| No WebGL errors | No shader/context errors | WebGL-specific errors |
| No React errors | No React warnings/errors | Component errors |

### Interaction Tests

| Criterion | PASS | FAIL |
|-----------|------|------|
| Pan works | Drag moves viewport | Pan stuck/broken |
| Zoom works | Scroll/buttons change zoom | Zoom stuck/broken |
| Sidebar syncs | Train IDs follow pan | Sidebar desynced |
| Timeline syncs | Dates follow pan | Timeline desynced |

---

## Test Result Documentation

After completing QA, document results in `claude-progress.txt`:

```markdown
**Visual Tests (Playwright MCP):**
- ✅/❌ [Description of what was tested]
- ✅/❌ [Another test result]
- ✅/❌ No console errors

**Files Changed:**
- [list of modified files]

**Commit:** [hash if committed]
```

---

## Harness Integration

The QA protocol integrates with the harness through:

### 1. Session Start Hook (`.claude/hooks/session-start.sh`)
- Reminds agent of work protocol including visual testing

### 2. End-of-Turn Hook (`.claude/hooks/end-of-turn-check.sh`)
- Runs typecheck on TypeScript changes
- Reminds about visual testing for canvas changes

### 3. Testing Protocol in `features.json`
```json
"testingProtocol": {
  "sequence": [
    "1. VISUAL TEST: Use Playwright MCP to verify UI renders correctly",
    "2. CONSOLE CHECK: Verify no errors in browser console",
    "3. QA TEST: Run QA checklist based on QA-PLAN-001 protocol",
    "4. MARK COMPLETE: Only set passes:true after all tests pass"
  ]
}
```

---

## Quick Reference Card

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│               QA QUICK REFERENCE                             │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                              │
│  BEFORE MARKING FEATURE COMPLETE:                           │
│                                                              │
│  □ bun run typecheck                    (TypeScript OK?)    │

│  □ LOD transitions tested               (Smooth changes?)   │
│  □ mcp__playwright__browser_close       (Cleanup)           │
│                                                              │
│  ALL CHECKS PASS? → Update features.json (passes: true)     │
│  ANY CHECK FAILS? → Fix issue, repeat QA                    │
│                                                              │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

toolUseResult.content:
# QA Testing Protocol for MMS Canvas

This document defines the mandatory Quality Assurance testing protocol for the MMS Canvas application. All features MUST pass this protocol before being marked as complete.

---

## Overview

The MMS Canvas is a WebGL-based visualization that requires specialized testing approaches beyond standard web app testing. This protocol ensures:

1. Visual correctness (WebGL renders properly)
2. Performance standards (60fps target)
3. Cross-browser compatibility
4. Console error-free execution
5. Consistent results across sessions

---

## Mandatory Test Sequence

Every feature that modifies `apps/canvas/` MUST follow this sequence:

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    QA TEST SEQUENCE                          │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                              │
│  1. TYPECHECK                                                │
│     └── bun run typecheck                                   │
│                                                              │
│  2. VISUAL TEST (Playwright MCP)                            │
│     ├── Navigate to http://localhost:5173                   │
│     ├── Take screenshot                                      │
│     ├── Verify content renders (not blank)                  │
│     └── Check layout matches expectations                    │
│                                                              │
│  3. CONSOLE CHECK                                           │
│     ├── Check for JavaScript errors                         │
│     ├── Check for WebGL warnings                            │
│     └── Check for React errors                              │
│                                                              │
│  4. INTERACTION TEST (if applicable)                        │
│     ├── Pan (drag to move)                                  │
│     ├── Zoom (scroll or buttons)                            │
│     ├── LOD transitions (MACRO ↔ MESO ↔ MICRO)             │
│     └── Sidebar sync                                         │
│                                                              │
│  5. PERFORMANCE CHECK (for F116+)                           │
│     ├── No visible lag during pan/zoom                      │
│     ├── Smooth LOD transitions                              │
│     └── Labels appear without delay                          │
│                                                              │
│  6. MARK COMPLETE                                           │
│     └── Update features.json (passes: true)                 │
│                                                              │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

---

## Playwright MCP Commands

### 1. Start Dev Server

Before running visual tests, ensure the dev server is running:

```bash
cd apps/canvas && bun run dev
```

### 2. Navigate to App

```
mcp__playwright__browser_navigate url="http://localhost:5173"
```

### 3. Check Console for Errors

```
mcp__playwright__browser_console_messages level="error"
```

**Interpretation:**
- Empty result or no red errors → PASS
- Any red errors → FAIL (investigate before continuing)
- Yellow warnings → Evaluate (may be acceptable)

### 4. Take Screenshot

```
mcp__playwright__browser_take_screenshot filename="screenshots/feature-name.png"
```

For full page:
```
mcp__playwright__browser_take_screenshot fullPage=true filename="screenshots/feature-full.png"
```

### 5. Accessibility Snapshot (Optional)

```
mcp__playwright__browser_snapshot
```

Useful for verifying:
- Element presence in DOM
- ARIA labels
- Interactive elements

### 6. Close Browser

```
mcp__playwright__browser_close
```

---

## WebGL-Specific Testing

### Configuration

The project uses Playwright MCP with headless mode configured in `.mcp.json`:

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "playwright": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["@playwright/mcp@latest", "--headless"]
    }
  }
}
```

**Key points:**
- `--headless` prevents browser window from stealing focus
- WebGL acceleration is enabled by default in Chromium
- Screenshots capture WebGL canvas content

### WebGL Verification Checklist

| Check | What to Look For | PASS | FAIL |
|-------|------------------|------|------|
| Canvas renders | Content visible (not blank) | Non-empty canvas | White/black screen |
| LOD layers | Correct layer for zoom level | Expected view type | Wrong layer |
| Colors | Correct task/heatmap colors | Blue/orange/gray/red | Missing or wrong |
| Performance | Smooth interactions | No visible lag | Choppy/slow |
| Console | No WebGL errors | Clean console | Shader/context errors |

### Common WebGL Issues

| Issue | Symptom | Solution |
|-------|---------|----------|
| Blank canvas | Nothing renders | Check InstancedMesh count, camera position |
| Wrong colors | Tasks not visible | Check material/color attributes |
| Shader error | Console shows GLSL error | Fix shader syntax |
| Context lost | Canvas goes blank after time | Handle contextlost event |
| Performance | Choppy zoom/pan | Check frustum culling, instance count |

---

## LOD Transition Testing

Test all three LOD levels and transitions:

### Zoom Levels

| LOD | Zoom Range | Expected View | Verification |
|-----|------------|---------------|--------------|
| MACRO | < 0.5x | Heatmap (yellow→red) | Cell colors visible |
| MESO | 0.5x - 2.0x | Task blocks (DNA pattern) | Colored rectangles |
| MICRO | > 2.0x | Task cards with labels | Grid lines, labels |

### Transition Tests

1. **MESO → MACRO:** Zoom out past 0.5x
   - Both layers should crossfade smoothly
   - No flickering

2. **MESO → MICRO:** Zoom in past 2.0x
   - Both layers should crossfade smoothly
   - Labels appear progressively

3. **Full range:** Zoom from 0.3x to 3.0x
   - All transitions smooth
   - No visual artifacts

---

## Approval Criteria Matrix

### Visual Tests

| Criterion | PASS | FAIL |
|-----------|------|------|
| Content renders | Canvas shows expected content | Blank/empty screen |
| Correct LOD | Layer matches zoom level | Wrong layer displayed |
| Colors correct | Task colors match type | Missing or wrong colors |
| Layout correct | Sidebar, header, canvas aligned | Misaligned elements |
| No artifacts | Clean rendering | Glitches, overlaps |

### Console Tests

| Criterion | PASS | FAIL |
|-----------|------|------|
| No JS errors | No red errors | Any red error |
| No WebGL errors | No shader/context errors | WebGL-specific errors |
| No React errors | No React warnings/errors | Component errors |

### Interaction Tests

| Criterion | PASS | FAIL |
|-----------|------|------|
| Pan works | Drag moves viewport | Pan stuck/broken |
| Zoom works | Scroll/buttons change zoom | Zoom stuck/broken |
| Sidebar syncs | Train IDs follow pan | Sidebar desynced |
| Timeline syncs | Dates follow pan | Timeline desynced |

---

## Test Result Documentation

After completing QA, document results in `claude-progress.txt`:

```markdown
**Visual Tests (Playwright MCP):**
- ✅/❌ [Description of what was tested]
- ✅/❌ [Another test result]
- ✅/❌ No console errors

**Files Changed:**
- [list of modified files]

**Commit:** [hash if committed]
```

---

## Harness Integration

The QA protocol integrates with the harness through:

### 1. Session Start Hook (`.claude/hooks/session-start.sh`)
- Reminds agent of work protocol including visual testing

### 2. End-of-Turn Hook (`.claude/hooks/end-of-turn-check.sh`)
- Runs typecheck on TypeScript changes
- Reminds about visual testing for canvas changes

### 3. Testing Protocol in `features.json`
```json
"testingProtocol": {
  "sequence": [
    "1. VISUAL TEST: Use Playwright MCP to verify UI renders correctly",
    "2. CONSOLE CHECK: Verify no errors in browser console",
    "3. QA TEST: Run QA checklist based on QA-PLAN-001 protocol",
    "4. MARK COMPLETE: Only set passes:true after all tests pass"
  ]
}
```

---

## Quick Reference Card

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│               QA QUICK REFERENCE                             │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                              │
│  BEFORE MARKING FEATURE COMPLETE:                           │
│                                                              │
│  □ bun run typecheck                    (TypeScript OK?)    │
│  □ mcp__playwright__browser_navigate    (Can access app?)   │
│  □ mcp__playwright__browser_take_screenshot (Content visible?)│
│  □ mcp__playwright__browser_console_messages (No errors?)   │
│  □ Pan/zoom tested                      (Interactions work?)│
│  □ LOD transitions tested               (Smooth changes?)   │
│  □ mcp__playwright__browser_close       (Cleanup)           │
│                                                              │
│  ALL CHECKS PASS? → Update features.json (passes: true)     │
│  ANY CHECK FAILS? → Fix issue, repeat QA                    │
│                                                              │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

---

## Sources

- [Headless Chrome WebGL Testing with Playwright](https://www.createit.com/blog/headless-chrome-testing-webgl-using-playwright/)
- [Playwright Visual Regression Testing](https://playwright.dev/docs/test-snapshots)
- [CSS-Tricks: Visual Regression Testing with Playwright](https://css-tricks.com/automated-visual-regression-testing-with-playwright/)

---

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/bin/bash
# Hook: Stop (Quality Gate)
# Purpose: End-of-turn validation - typecheck, visual test reminders
# Exit 2 = BLOCK continuation, Exit 0 = Allow

PROJECT_DIR="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-$(pwd)}"
cd "$PROJECT_DIR" 2>/dev/null || exit 0

echo "━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━"
echo "🔍 END-OF-TURN QUALITY CHECK"
echo "━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━"

# 1. TypeScript check (if .ts/.tsx changed)
TS_CHANGED=false
if git diff --name-only HEAD 2>/dev/null | grep -qE '\.(ts|tsx)$'; then
  TS_CHANGED=true
fi
if git ls-files --others --exclude-standard 2>/dev/null | grep -qE '\.(ts|tsx)$'; then
  TS_CHANGED=true
fi

if [ "$TS_CHANGED" = true ]; then
  echo "📝 TypeScript files changed - running typecheck..."
  if ! bun run typecheck 2>&1 | head -20; then
    echo "" >&2
    echo "❌ TYPECHECK FAILED" >&2
    echo "Fix TypeScript errors before continuing." >&2
    exit 2
  fi
  echo "✅ Typecheck passed"
fi

# 2. Check for canvas app changes (visual test reminder)
CANVAS_CHANGED=false
if git diff --name-only HEAD 2>/dev/null | grep -q 'apps/canvas/'; then
  CANVAS_CHANGED=true
fi
if git ls-files --others --exclude-standard 2>/dev/null | grep -q 'apps/canvas/'; then
  CANVAS_CHANGED=true
fi

if [ "$CANVAS_CHANGED" = true ]; then
  echo ""
  echo "┌────────────────────────────────────────────┐"
  echo "│ 📸 VISUAL TEST REQUIRED                    │"
  echo "│                                            │"
  echo "│ Canvas app changed. Before marking feature │"
  echo "│ complete, run visual test with Playwright: │"
  echo "│                                            │"
  echo "│ 1. mcp__playwright__browser_navigate       │"
  echo "│ 2. mcp__playwright__browser_console_messages│"
  echo "│ 3. mcp__playwright__browser_take_screenshot│"
  echo "│ 4. Analyze screenshot                      │"
  echo "└────────────────────────────────────────────┘"
fi

# 3. Harness file validation
if [ ! -f "features.json" ]; then
  echo "⚠️  features.json not found - harness incomplete" >&2
fi

if [ ! -f "claude-progress.txt" ]; then
  echo "⚠️  claude-progress.txt not found - update progress log" >&2
fi

echo ""
echo "✅ End-of-turn check complete"
exit 0

#!/bin/bash
# Hook: SessionStart - Harness Context Injection + Self-Correction
# Purpose: Ensure Claude understands harness concept at session start
# Always exit 0 (informational only)

PROJECT_DIR="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-$(pwd)}"
cd "$PROJECT_DIR" 2>/dev/null || exit 0

echo "━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━"
echo "🚀 MMS HARNESS - SESSION START"
echo "━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━"

# WHAT IS A HARNESS? (definition FIRST - before any steps)
echo ""
echo "┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐"
echo "│ WHAT IS A HARNESS?                                          │"
echo "├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤"
echo "│ A 'Harness' is a framework that enables AI agents to work   │"
echo "│ on long tasks across multiple context windows/sessions.     │"
echo "│                                                              │"
echo "│ PROBLEM: Each new session starts with NO memory.            │"
echo "│ SOLUTION: State files (progress + features) persist context.│"
echo "│                                                              │"
echo "│ Official source: anthropic.com/engineering/                 │"
echo "│                  effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents│"
echo "└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘"

# Current date (prevents stale training data issue)
echo ""
echo "📅 Current date: $(date '+%Y-%m-%d')"

# Work protocol
echo ""
echo "┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐"
echo "│ WORK PROTOCOL                                               │"
echo "├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤"
echo "│ 1. READ claude-progress.txt → session history              │"
echo "│ 2. READ features.json → next feature (passes:false)        │"
echo "│ 3. IMPLEMENT one feature at a time                          │"
echo "│ 4. TEST visually with Playwright MCP                        │"
echo "│ 5. COMMIT after feature passes                              │"
echo "│ 6. UPDATE claude-progress.txt                               │"
echo "└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘"

# SELF-CORRECTION PROTOCOL (key innovation)
echo ""
echo "┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐"
echo "│ 🔄 SELF-CORRECTION PROTOCOL                                 │"
echo "├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤"
echo "│ If you DON'T KNOW what a 'Harness' is:                      │"
echo "│                                                              │"
echo "│ 1. SEARCH: WebSearch 'Anthropic harness agents 2025'        │"
echo "│ 2. ANALYZE: Why was local documentation unclear?            │"
echo "│ 3. FIX: Update CLAUDE.md or hooks to improve clarity        │"
echo "│ 4. DOCUMENT: Record the fix in claude-progress.txt          │"
echo "│                                                              │"
echo "│ This is a CONTINUOUS IMPROVEMENT system.                    │"
echo "│ Each session should leave harness clearer for the next.     │"
echo "└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘"

# Package versions (helps with stale knowledge detection)
# Note: dependencies are in apps/canvas/package.json, not root
if [ -f "apps/canvas/package.json" ]; then
  echo ""
  echo "📦 Current package versions (apps/canvas):"
  jq -r '.dependencies | to_entries[] | select(.key | test("react|three|blueprint")) | "   \(.key): \(.value)"' apps/canvas/package.json 2>/dev/null || true
fi

# Recent git activity
if git rev-parse --git-dir > /dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo ""
  echo "📜 Recent commits:"
  git --no-pager log --oneline -3 2>/dev/null | sed 's/^/   /'
fi

exit 0

file.content:
# MMS System Documentation

This directory contains domain knowledge, requirements, research, and UX specifications for the Metro Integrated Planning System.

## Document Index

| Document | Description | Audience |
|----------|-------------|----------|
| [DOMAIN.md](./DOMAIN.md) | Fleet composition, maintenance types, domain entities | Developers, Domain Experts |
| [REQUIREMENTS.md](./REQUIREMENTS.md) | Business requirements (from RFQ) | Product, Engineering |
| [UX_SPECIFICATION.md](./UX_SPECIFICATION.md) | UX design and interaction patterns | Designers, Frontend Devs |
| [RESEARCH.md](./RESEARCH.md) | Industry research on maintenance scheduling | All stakeholders |

## Project Context

### What is MMS?

The **Metro Integrated Planning System (MMS)** is a high-performance visualization tool for railway rolling stock maintenance planning. It provides a "Single Pane of Glass" solution that unifies:

- **Strategic Planning** (1-12 years): Budget allocation, major overhauls
- **Tactical Planning** (weeks-months): Workload balancing, resource leveling
- **Operational Planning** (daily): Run assignments, track allocation

### The "Semantic Zoom" Concept

Inspired by Google Earth, the system implements **semantic zoom** - the visualization automatically transforms based on zoom level:

| Zoom Level | Threshold | Time Scale | Visualization |
|------------|-----------|------------|---------------|
| Macro | < 0.5x | 10-20 years | Heatmap (yellow→orange→red) |
| Meso | 0.5-2x | 2-5 years | Task blocks (DNA pattern) |
| Micro | > 2x | 4-8 weeks | Detailed cards with labels |

### Current Implementation

**Production:** `apps/canvas` - React 18 + R3F + BlueprintJS 6 app with semantic zoom.

```bash
# Run production app (http://localhost:5173)
cd apps/canvas && bun run dev
```

**Production Features (F101-F117):**

| Phase | Features | Description |
|-------|----------|-------------|
| **Foundation** | F101-F103 | Vite + React 18 + BlueprintJS + R3F setup |
| **Core Rendering** | F104-F107 | Data, MesoLayer, pan/zoom, LOD state |
| **All Layers** | F108-F110 | MacroLayer, MicroLayer, smooth transitions |
| **UI Integration** | F111-F114 | Sidebar, timeline, controls, conflicts |
| **Polish** | F115-F117 | Instructions, performance, visual parity |

**POC Reference:**

The POC (`design/reference/semantic-zoom-poc.html`) serves as visual reference only:
- Visual appearance of semantic zoom UX
- 3 LOD levels (Macro/Meso/Micro)
- Sidebar and timeline layout
- Conflict visual indicators

See `features.json` for complete feature list and status.

## Relationship to Implementation

### Production App (`apps/canvas`)

The UX patterns in [UX_SPECIFICATION.md](./UX_SPECIFICATION.md) are implemented in:
- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/` - R3F rendering components
- `apps/canvas/src/components/` - BlueprintJS UI components
- `apps/canvas/src/hooks/` - State management (useViewport)

### Domain Entities

The domain entities in [DOMAIN.md](./DOMAIN.md) are implemented in:
- `apps/canvas/src/data/types.ts` - TypeScript interfaces
- `apps/canvas/src/data/generator.ts` - Mock data generation

### POC Reference

The validated prototype (`design/reference/semantic-zoom-poc.html`) serves as visual reference only:
- Demonstrates semantic zoom concept visually
- Visual reference for production implementation
- Shows what the 3 LOD levels should look like

### Future Development

The full RFQ requirements in [REQUIREMENTS.md](./REQUIREMENTS.md) include features not yet implemented:
- Real-time mileage data integration
- What-If scenario simulation
- Cost forecasting and budget tracking
- IBM Maximo integration

These represent the product roadmap beyond the current demo.

## Quick Links

- **Technical Architecture**: See `agent_docs/architecture.md`
- **Visual References**: See `design/README.md`
- **Code Conventions**: See `agent_docs/conventions.md`
- **Session Progress**: See `claude-progress.txt`

{
  "project": "Metro Integrated Planning System (MMS)",
  "version": "0.3.0",
  "lastUpdated": "2025-12-16",
  "product": {
    "entry": "apps/canvas",
    "description": "Production React app with semantic zoom (Vite + React 18 + R3F + BlueprintJS 6)"
  },
  "reference": {
    "entry": "design/reference/semantic-zoom-poc.html",
    "description": "Standalone POC for visual reference only"
  },
  "priorityQueue": {
    "description": "Features ordered by execution priority. Execute in order, respecting blockedBy dependencies.",
    "note": "Priority 0 = immediate, Priority 1 = high, Priority 2 = after dependencies"
  },
  "features": [
    {
      "id": "HARNESS-001",
      "category": "harness",
      "priority": 0,
      "description": "Configure Playwright MCP for headless without focus stealing",
      "steps": [
        "Research Playwright headless configuration options",
        "Update MCP settings for headless mode",
        "Test that browser windows don't steal focus during visual tests",
        "Document configuration in CLAUDE.md"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": null,
      "notes": "COMPLETE - Playwright MCP configured with --headless flag in .mcp.json. Deny permissions added to block access to personal directories."
    },
    {
      "id": "QA-PLAN-001",
      "category": "harness",
      "priority": 0,
      "description": "Plan QA testing protocol with deep thinking (planning mode)",
      "steps": [
        "Enter planning mode with deep thinking enabled",
        "Research QA best practices for canvas/WebGL applications",
        "Design protocol to enforce QA tests AFTER visual tests",
        "Define QA checklist based on UX-AUDIT-001 discoveries",

        "Document in docs/QA_PROTOCOL.md"
